reasoning: A 1% increase from an Elo of 2816 requires a gain of roughly 28 Elo points, which would put Nakamura at ~2844. Historically, only Magnus Carlsen, Garry Kasparov, and Fabiano Caruana have reached or exceeded this rating in standard classical chess. For an elite top-5 player to gain 28 points in a brief 1 to 3-month timeframe is exceptionally rare, requiring a historic tournament performance. Additionally, FIDE ratings on Wikipedia's 'FIDE rankings' page are typically updated monthly. Because of this, the rating on November 16 will mathematically almost certainly be identical to the rating on November 9. Even spanning out to December 2025 and February 2026, the probability of reaching 2844 by stringing together multiple overwhelming victories against 2750+ opponents is incredibly small. Given the base rate of 0.007 and a minimum allowed probability of 0.02, I am providing estimates clustered near the absolute lower bound, with a fractionally higher probability for the furthest date to reflect the marginally longer time window for tournaments to take place.
